sections.content:
Blue is at pixel (15, 21) which is grid[5][7]. Green is at pixel (45, 45) which is grid[15][15]. So the player moved from grid[5][5] to grid[5][6] to grid[5][7]. Wait, the blue started at (15, 15) which is grid[5][5]. After RIGHT, it's at (15, 21) which is grid[5][7]. So each move is 6 pixels. So the grid is 6x6 cells per block. Let me recompute: 15// 6:
Blue: (15, 21) -> grid[2][3]
Green: (45, 45) -> grid[7][7]
Wait, that doesn't match. Let me recalculate:
15 // 6 = 2 (with remainder 3)
21 // 6 = 3 (with remainder 3)
45 // 6 = 7 (with remainder 3)
45 // 6 = 7 (with remainder 3)
So blue is at grid[2][3] and green is at grid[7][7]. But the maze is 11x11, not 8x8. Let me reconsider.
Actually, the pixel coordinates are (15, 21) for blue and (45, 45) for green. The grid is 64x64. If each cell is 6 pixels, then the grid is 64/6 = 10.67 cells. That doesn't work.
Let me try 3 pixels per cell: 15/ 3 = 5, 21 / 3 = 7, 45 / 3 = 15, 45 / 3 = 15. So blue is at grid[5][7] and green is at grid[15][15]. So the grid is 64/3 = 21.33 cells. That doesn't work either.
Let me try to figure out the cell size. The blue token is at (15, 21). The green token is at (45, 45). The difference is (30, 30). So the grid is 10x10 cells, and each cell is 3 pixels. So the grid is 60x60 cells, and the grid is 60x60, with 4 pixels of padding on each side. So the maze is 10x10 cells.
Wait, but the blue token is at (15, 21) which is grid[5][7] if the maze starts at (15, 15). But the blue token started at (15, 15) which is grid[5][5]. After RIGHT, it's at (15, 21) which is grid[5][7]. So each move is 6 pixels. So the cell size is 6x6 pixels.
So the grid is 64x64, with the maze starting at (15, 15) and ending at (45, 45). The maze is 30x30 pixels, which is 5x5 cells if each cell is 6x6 pixels.
Wait, but the maze is 11x11 in my earlier grid. Let me reconsider.
Actually, let me just try to navigate. The blue is at (15, 21) and the green is at (45, 45). I need to go DOWN and RIGHT to reach the green. Let me try a sequence of moves.
Actually, let me just try to navigate the maze is a simple maze. Let me try to navigate by trial and error. Let me try DOWN first.
